name: BRONCHIAL ADENOMA
definition: misnomer due to locally invasive features + tendency for recurrence + occasional metastasis ADENOID CYSTIC CARCINOMA (7%) MIXED TYPE (extremely rare)

demographics.Path: arises from duct epithelium of bronchial mucous glands (predominant distribution of
demographics.Mean age: 35-45 (range, 12-60) years; 90% occur < 50 years of age; most common primary

distribution: hilar enlargement / mediastinal widening = central endo- / exobronchial mass
discriminating_features: complete obstruction / air trapping in partial obstruction (rare) / nonobstructive (10-15%)
recurrent postobstructive infection: pneumonitis, bronchiectasis, abscess
atelectasis / consolidation of a lung / lobe / segment (78%)
collateral air drift may prevent atelectasis

general: complete obstruction / air trapping in partial obstruction (rare) / nonobstructive (10-15%)
recurrent postobstructive infection: pneumonitis, bronchiectasis, abscess
atelectasis / consolidation of a lung / lobe / segment (78%)
collateral air drift may prevent atelectasis
solitary round / oval slightly lobulated pulmonary nodule (19%) of 1-10 cm in size
hilar enlargement / mediastinal widening = central endo- / exobronchial mass
CT: in close proximity to an adjacent bifurcation with splaying of bronchus
coarse peripheral calcifications in ⅓ (cartilaginous / bony transformation)
may involve trachea = locally invasive tumor

clinical.Prognosis: 95% 5-year survival rate; 75% 15-year survival rate after resection
